Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE AND RE-OPEN EXPERT DISCOVERY

No objections made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion to continue the trial date and re-open expert discovery for the limited purpose of replacing plaintiffs' expert Dr. Herman Bruch is granted. The trial date is continued to August 17, 2015 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Expert discovery is re-opened solely to allow the following: plaintiffs may designate an expert in place of Dr. Bruch and such expert may prepare a report and be presented for deposition; if needed, defendants may supplement their expert witness designation limited to responding to plaintiffs' new designation. This re-opened discovery closes on the new trial date. All other expert and fact discovery remains closed. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Apr-22-2015 Continued To Aug-17-15 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Todd A. Duplanty of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Superior Boiler Works, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Brandie Pittman, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT SUPERIOR BOILER WORKS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Superior Boiler Works, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2, 3, 5 and 6, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Anthony Hale, Sr. was exposed to products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Finigan's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Assuming that Mr. Helfrich's declaration is admissible, it is insufficient to demonstrate that Mr. Hale did not work with/around a Superior Boiler Works boiler. When read in conjunction with Mr. Finigan's testimony, it merely creates a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's motion did not raise the issue of asbestos content of any products or materials for which it is responsible, but even if it did, the testimony of Mr. Helfrich would create a triable issue whether the gaskets identified by Mr. Finigan to which Mr. Hale was exposed contained asbestos. Summary adjudication issue 4 (false representation) is denied as moot because a review of plaintiffs' complaint demonstrates that cause of action was not alleged against defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Finigan's declaration are overruled. Had defendant properly raised the issue of asbestos content, defendant's objections to its General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses and to the prior testimony of Robert Helfrich would be overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
